enonce.texte:
Exercise 1: Match the modal verb to its primary function.
Read each sentence and choose the correct function from the list below.
Functions: ability / obligation / advice / possibility / polite request

questions.texte: You must wear a uniform at school.
She can speak three languages fluently.
It might rain later, so take an umbrella.
You should see a doctor if your fever continues.
Could you help me carry these bags, please?

enonce.texte:
Exercise 2: Complete each sentence with the correct modal verb.
Choose from: can / could / must / should / may / might / would (some modals may be used more than once, but each blank has one correct answer).

questions.texte: In Cameroon, you _______ vote when you are 20 years old. (ability/right)
The doctor said: 'You _______ reduce your salt intake to lower your blood pressure.' (advice)
_______ you mind opening the window? It's very hot in here. (polite request)
Students _______ not cheat during the national exam; it is strictly forbidden. (obligation/prohibition)
Where is my pen? It _______ be in my bag, or I _______ have left it in the classroom. (possibility – two blanks)

intitule: Competence Exercise: Real-Life Problem Solving at School

enonce.texte:
Exercise 3: School rules and advice.
Read the situations below. Using modals, write one sentence for each situation: one expressing obligation (must) and one expressing advice (should).

questions.texte: Situation: During a storm, a student wants to stand under a tall tree.
questions.reponse: Obligation: Students must not stand under tall trees during a storm. Advice: You should go inside the classroom immediately.

sousQuestions.reponse: Students must not stand under tall trees during a storm.
You should go inside the classroom immediately.

questions.texte: Situation: A classmate has a sore throat and a fever but wants to play football in the sun.
questions.reponse: Obligation: You must rest and avoid physical effort. Advice: You should see the school nurse or drink warm tea with honey.

sousQuestions.reponse: You must rest and avoid physical effort.
You should see the school nurse or drink warm tea with honey.

intitule: Competence Exercise: Community and Civic Life

enonce.texte:
Exercise 4: Civic problems – suggestions and obligations.
You are a youth representative in your village council. Use modals to respond to each problem. Write one sentence for each problem using the modal indicated in brackets.

questions.texte: Problem: Some neighbours burn plastic waste, causing air pollution. (must / prohibition)
questions.reponse: People must not burn plastic waste because it harms the lungs and the environment.

questions.texte: Problem: Teenagers in your community are not attending literacy classes. (should / advice)
questions.reponse: Teenagers should attend literacy classes to improve their future job opportunities.

questions.texte: Problem: A neighbour cannot afford medicine for malaria. (could / polite suggestion for help)
questions.reponse: We could organise a small community fundraiser to help buy the medicine.

questions.texte: Problem: You need to ask the chief for permission to organise a clean-up day. (may / polite request)
questions.reponse: May we organise a clean-up day next Saturday to remove waste from the village centre?

intitule: Competence Exercise: Analysing a Dialogue (HIV/AIDS Awareness Context)

enonce.texte:
Exercise 5: Analysing modals in a health dialogue.
Read the conversation between a nurse and a student. Then answer the questions using Bloom's taxonomy (analyse/evaluate).

Dialogue:
Nurse: You should get tested for HIV/AIDS regularly. It's free at this clinic.
Student: But I'm scared. The results might be positive.
Nurse: You must not let fear stop you. Early treatment can help you live a long life.
Student: Could I bring a friend for support?
Nurse: Of course you can. And you must tell a trusted adult if you need help.
Student: What if someone sees me here?
Nurse: You could wear a mask or come after school hours. Your privacy is important.

questions.texte: Identify two different modal verbs in the dialogue and explain their functions (analyse).
questions.reponse: 1. 'should' (advice) – the nurse advises regular testing. 2. 'must not' (prohibition/strong obligation) – the nurse prohibits letting fear stop you. Also 'can' (ability/possibility) – early treatment can help.

questions.texte: Evaluate the nurse's advice: 'You could wear a mask or come after school hours.' Is this a good solution for a student in a small Cameroonian village? Justify your answer using modals.
questions.reponse: This solution may work, but it might not be perfect. In a small village, people could recognise you even with a mask. A better solution might be to have a mobile clinic or to educate the community so that students should not feel ashamed. However, coming after school hours could be a good temporary solution if the clinic is open.

questions.texte: Rewrite the student's last sentence as a polite request using 'would' or 'could' without changing the meaning.
questions.reponse: Would you please keep my visit confidential? or Could you please ensure my privacy?
